thesis.label: Capability Thesis
thesis.title: From Point Tools to Enterprise Production Infrastructure
thesis.description: What Zeta builds for enterprises is not a single agent or isolated scenario automation, but a composable, collaborative, self-iterating, and governable enterprise-grade agent operating capability system. This system connects models, knowledge, systems, workflows, and people into an agent cluster network that continuously executes, learns, and optimizes — ultimately becoming the enterprise's new production infrastructure.

contrasts.from: Adding a chat box to systems
contrasts.to: Abstracting cognitive work into executable agents

items.name: Agent Encapsulation
items.definition: Encapsulate work units scattered across people, documents, systems, and processes into callable, composable, evaluable, and reusable agent capability units.
items.frontier: This is the first step from functional digitization to workforce digitization.
items.landing: Sales follow-up agent, recruitment screening agent, customer service agent, project delivery agent, knowledge assistant agent, data analysis agent
items.essence: Transform job capabilities, process actions, and knowledge decisions into deployable agent units

items.name: Orchestration & Cluster Collaboration
items.definition: Enable multiple agents to coordinate around tasks through division, collaboration, routing, relay, and supervision — forming a controllable agent orchestration network.
items.frontier: Truly advanced enterprise AI isn't one super agent doing everything, but multiple specialized agents collaborating within clear boundaries.
items.landing: Controller agent decomposes tasks, knowledge agent queries policies, data agent pulls reports, workflow agent connects OA/CRM/ERP, review agent ensures quality
items.essence: Upgrade point intelligence into collaborative organizational intelligence

items.name: Business Execution Loop
items.definition: Move agents from 'can answer' to 'can execute, write back, and track results' — truly becoming part of business processes.
items.frontier: The real value lies in moving from Assistive AI to Operational AI.
items.landing: Auto-query system data, auto-generate/update work orders, auto-fill forms and trigger approvals, auto-push messages and assign tasks
items.essence: Agents become execution-layer production units, not just cognitive tools

items.name: Self-Iteration & Optimization
items.definition: Enable agent systems to continuously optimize based on feedback, results, human corrections, and business metrics — not static after deployment.
items.frontier: Leading clients care not about 'can it go live' but 'will it keep getting better and more aligned with real business'.
items.landing: Optimize prompts based on user feedback, improve routing from task history, crystallize rules from human corrections
items.essence: Give enterprise AI systems operational learning capabilities

items.name: Human-AI Co-Governance
items.definition: Agent clusters operate with permission control, audit trails, quality assessment, human override, and risk boundaries — not as black boxes.
items.frontier: The more enterprises understand AI, the more they care about permissions, auditing, override capabilities, and governance integration.
items.landing: Unified identity and permissions, operation logs and audit trails, human approval nodes, high-risk task interception, output quality assessment
items.essence: Transform agents from experimental tools to trusted production systems

items.name: Intelligent Operations
items.definition: Enable enterprises to manage agent networks like digital systems — monitoring cost, effectiveness, coverage, and ROI.
items.frontier: Advanced clients ultimately want not to buy agents, but to build an AI Operating Model.
items.landing: Track deployed agents, completed process loops, saved human-hours, response speed improvement, task success/failure rates
items.essence: Turn AI from projects into long-term enterprise operating assets

orchestration.title: Agent Orchestration & Execution
orchestration.subtitle: Task entry → Decomposition → Routing → System/Knowledge/Process calls → Quality check → Human override → Write-back & logging
points: The controller agent is a dispatcher, not the sole executor
Multiple agents operate within clear boundaries
Humans retain approval, override, and final responsibility at key nodes
Results write back to systems, crystallize knowledge, and feed monitoring

governance.title: Governance & Intelligent Operations
governance.subtitle: Not 'can it be done' but 'can it be operated long-term'

hero.label: Case Library
hero.title: Proving Agent Value Through Real Deployment
hero.subtitle: See how Zeta brings agent capability systems to life through real cases, customer stories, and interactive demos

cases.title: Intelligent Sales Follow-up System
cases.background: The sales team was overwhelmed managing a high volume of leads with low manual follow-up efficiency.
cases.problem: Slow lead response, inconsistent follow-up standards, customer profiles scattered across multiple systems.
cases.solution: Deployed a multi-agent collaborative sales system: lead assignment agent for auto-distribution, follow-up agent for personalized outreach, data agent for unified customer profiles.
cases.result: 80% faster lead response, sales teams can focus on high-value clients, 35% improvement in conversion rate.

cases.title: Enterprise Knowledge Hub Agent
cases.background: Enterprise knowledge was scattered across documents, policies, emails, and multiple business systems.
cases.problem: Difficult cross-department knowledge retrieval, slow onboarding, knowledge base lagging behind policy updates.
cases.solution: Built a knowledge hub agent cluster: indexing agent for continuous updates, retrieval agent for multi-path recall, compliance agent for output accuracy.
cases.result: 5x improvement in cross-department query efficiency, 95%+ accuracy, 40% reduction in new hire onboarding time.

cases.title: Multi-Agent Approval System
cases.background: Complex group-level approval processes involving multi-department cross-review, compliance checks, and risk assessment.
cases.problem: Long approval cycles, human review oversight, siloed information across stages.
cases.solution: Built a multi-agent approval flow: decomposition agent for type identification, compliance agent for auto-verification, risk agent for anomaly flagging, flow agent for process driving.
cases.result: 60% shorter approval cycles, 98% anomaly interception rate, full process traceability.

items.title: From Manual Dispatching to Agent Auto-Scheduling
items.quote: We used to spend two hours daily on work order assignments. Now the agent handles distribution automatically — I only deal with exceptions.

items.title: Knowledge Queries: From Hours to Seconds
items.quote: Looking up a policy used to mean searching through multiple systems. Now I just ask the agent and get an accurate answer with source citations.

items.title: Why Enterprises Need an Agent Capability System, Not Point Tools
items.summary: From single AI tools to building organization-level AI workforce systems — enterprises need more than tools, they need a complete capability system.

items.title: From Copilot to Operational AI: The Next Step for Enterprise AI
items.summary: When AI evolves from assistive tools to production systems that can independently execute business loops, enterprise AI strategy needs a fundamental upgrade.

items.title: How to Evaluate if Your Enterprise is Ready for Agents
items.summary: Not all scenarios are suitable for agent deployment. This article provides a framework to identify the best entry points.

items.title: Zeta Completes New Funding Round to Accelerate Enterprise Agent Deployment
items.summary: The funding will expand the technical team and accelerate product development.

items.title: Zeta Enters Strategic Partnership with Leading Manufacturer
items.summary: Both parties will collaborate deeply on intelligent sales, supply chain coordination, and more.

items.q: What types of business are suitable for agents?
items.a: Knowledge-intensive and process-driven work with high repetition, clear rules, and multi-system coordination. Examples include customer service, sales follow-up, approval workflows, and knowledge retrieval.

items.q: How does Zeta start a project?
items.a: We typically begin with business diagnosis to identify the most valuable agent opportunities, run a pilot, then gradually expand to more processes and departments.

items.q: Can agents integrate with existing enterprise systems?
items.a: Yes. Zeta's agent system can integrate with CRM, ERP, OA, knowledge bases, and other mainstream enterprise systems for data retrieval, process triggers, and result write-back.
Zeta supports private deployment with all data flowing within the enterprise. We provide comprehensive permission control, audit trails, and data encryption.
Pilot projects typically launch in 4-8 weeks, covering requirements analysis, agent design, system integration, testing, and deployment. Continuous iteration follows.

items.q: How does the demo differ from production projects?
items.a: Demos showcase core interaction and capability logic. Production projects integrate with real enterprise systems, knowledge, and workflows, including permissions, monitoring, and operations.

beliefs.title: Capability System Over Point Tools
beliefs.desc: Enterprises need not a few AI tools, but a continuously operable agent capability system.

beliefs.title: Deployment Over Concepts
beliefs.desc: We focus on whether agents can truly run in business, produce results, and be continuously used.

beliefs.title: Governance Over Automation
beliefs.desc: Automation without governance is uncontrollable. Enterprise AI must have permissions, auditing, and human override.

beliefs.title: Iteration Over One-Time Delivery
beliefs.desc: True agent systems should become more accurate and better adapted to real business over time.
